title: Is this Map Level 2 or 3 tier Canon
display_title: Is this Map Level 2 or 3 tier Canon
wikitext:
[]: Is this Map Level 2- or 3-tier Canon?             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Aug 07, 2010 1:51 pm Not sure where to talk about this but I picked up this map (1:25,000 scale) from the Gigazine site: [URL=http://i34.photobucket.com/albums/d135/soulassassin547/eva/eva_hakone_map14.jpg] [/URL] http://en.gigazine.net/index.php?/news/comments/20090605_eva_legwork01/ http://en.gigazine.net/index.php?/news/comments/20090605_eva_legwork02/ Which led me wondering if the map is considered canon material... Anyway, places marked in red with numbers indicate important locales and landmarks found in the show. As the kanji is way too small to see, we'll never know where's the future site of Misato's condo block. Two long lines mark out the main defense lines, Gora and Komagatake. One big circle shows just how large is the Geofront belowground (but looks it should take some cues from the 100% Newtype artbook). [URL=http://i34.photobucket.com/albums/d135/soulassassin547/eva/map_hakone-region.gif] [/URL] Looks like a bit of editing will have to be worked out on this one.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Aug 07, 2010 2:41 pm I'd rank it at about a 2.74.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Aug 07, 2010 8:32 pm I'd place it between Puchi Eva and the Kozo's Haunted Mansion video game on the tiers of canonicity. So, about five notches above the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Aug 03, 2013 11:52 pm Please tell me there's a nice big scan of that Hakone map somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Aug 04, 2013 5:23 am I think it's just there so, if you're in Japan and wanna visit the places where cool Eva scenes were set in, you can go there. It's got more to do with being a fun part of reality than a piece of some fiction's canon.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Aug 07, 2013 4:32 pm That's a pretty awesome map. All we need now is a translation of it, and we're all set! ;)
